caveats: a: The BLS source covers all fabricated metal products rather than the narrowed industrial-container lens.
a: The frozen compensation ratio covers the full heterogeneous NAICS code, including excluded activities.
rho: General manufacturing surveys may not represent smaller drum and pail manufacturers.
rho: Some operational improvements attributed to AI may actually be conventional automation or lean manufacturing.
e: The frozen firm count is margin-bridged and spans the full heterogeneous NAICS code.
e: Reconditioners are generally classified outside 332439 and must not be counted merely because they handle the same containers.
s5: Gallup is cross-industry and measures owner plans rather than completed control transfers.
s5: Some sales are individual plants or assets rather than transferable lens firms.
q: Greif is a global diversified supplier rather than a target-scale firm.
q: Capture varies by standard drum, specialty lining or closure, short-run pail, and customer concentration.
d5: Greif's segment is global and its reported dollar volume effect is not a direct constant-quality unit index.
d5: The narrowed lens excludes consumer and air-cargo categories that may have different demand trajectories.
o: The estimate includes elimination by alternative formats and reuse, not only software self-service.
o: A shift to another manufacturer does not reduce operator requirement even if it harms an incumbent.

envelope.interval_scope: research-input sensitivity with dataset anchors (l, n) held fixed

lens.customer_and_revenue_model: Repeat industrial packaging is supplied through purchase orders and master supply relationships, with pricing sensitive to steel, freight, capacity, specifications, quality, and service; many customers reorder standardized or configured containers as their own production volumes require.
lens.deviation_from_default: Narrowed to industrial light-gauge shipping containers because the NAICS code combines recurring drums, barrels, and pails with consumer durables and air-cargo containers whose customers, production economics, demand drivers, and transferability are too different for one coherent screen.
lens.excluded_activities: Captive plants, shells, non-control financing vehicles, firms outside the EBITDA band, pure distributors and reconditioners without qualifying manufacturing, and consumer-oriented lunch boxes, tool boxes, mailboxes, garbage cans, vacuum bottles, and specialized air-cargo containers.
lens.included_activities: US lower-middle-market independent manufacturers supplying recurring industrial light-gauge metal shipping containers, principally steel drums, barrels, pails, bins, and vats, to external chemical, petroleum, coatings, agricultural, food, and other industrial customers.

a.bridge: BLS reports occupations for broader NAICS 332, not wage-weighted tasks in industrial 332439 firms. I map physical pressing, forming, welding, coating, assembly, line tending, material handling, and maintenance to low AI substitutability, while assigning higher exposure to inspection, scheduling, demand planning, purchasing, specification work, customer service, and administration. The proxy may overstate exposure if drum lines are already highly automated or understate it in small-batch pail and specialty-container shops.
caveats: The BLS source covers all fabricated metal products rather than the narrowed industrial-container lens.
The frozen compensation ratio covers the full heterogeneous NAICS code, including excluded activities.
a.change_evidence: A lens-specific occupational wage mix and time studies at independent drum and pail plants separating direct line, changeover, maintenance, quality, warehouse, planning, commercial, and administrative tasks would materially refine exposure.
a.rationale: Direct container production remains physical and substantially mechanized. The not-yet-automated opportunity is concentrated in machine vision, maintenance analytics, production and inventory planning, quality documentation, quoting, order entry, procurement, and indirect labor.

d5.bridge: Greif's global Durable Metal Solutions segment is a large-company proxy dominated by steel drums but not limited to US LMM 332439 firms. Its comparable eleven-month 2025 sales fell by $99.6 million, including $70.7 million attributed to lower volume, after a modest volume increase in fiscal 2024. I translate that cyclical weakness into a roughly flat five-year constant-price quantity base with wide recovery and substitution bounds. The proxy may understate niche US demand or overstate exposure to global industrial cycles.
caveats: Greif's segment is global and its reported dollar volume effect is not a direct constant-quality unit index.
The narrowed lens excludes consumer and air-cargo categories that may have different demand trajectories.
d5.change_evidence: Five-year US shipment data for new steel drums and pails from the Industrial Steel Drum Institute, Census product shipments, and customer-sector production forecasts, separated from reconditioned containers, would materially change the ratio.
d5.rationale: Chemical, petroleum, coatings, agricultural, and other industrial output creates repeat packaging demand, but container volume is cyclically tied to customer production. Plastic drums, fiber drums, intermediate bulk containers, reuse and reconditioning, lightweighting, and bulk logistics can displace new metal units.

caveats: The frozen firm count is margin-bridged and spans the full heterogeneous NAICS code.
Reconditioners are generally classified outside 332439 and must not be counted merely because they handle the same containers.
e.change_evidence: A firm-level classification of the 66 dataset-estimated LMM firms by product, ownership, manufacturing versus reconditioning, captive status, customer concentration, certifications, and management depth would materially change eligibility.
e.rationale: Industrial drums, barrels, and pails support transferable employee-based merchant firms, and Greif describes competition from numerous smaller companies. Eligibility is reduced because the NAICS count also includes excluded consumer and cargo products, while some industrial operations are captive, reconditioning-led, strategically large, or too owner- and customer-dependent.

l.detail: QCEW 2024 private total annual wages at 332439 ($1,010,753,413) x benefits multiplier 1.4294 [ECEC private-industry total comp $46.60/hr / wages $32.60/hr = 1.4294 (March 2026 reference period)] / SUSB 2022 receipts at 332439 ($4,603,741,000). Vintage mismatch: 2024 wages over 2022 receipts, stated. Level: 6-digit. Harmonized to IPS basis ÷1.3407 (median of 65 dual-method codes).

n.detail: margin 0.1962 (margins.json[33]: Damodaran Jan 2026: Machinery EBITDA/Sales 19.62%); revenue/employee $444,419 (SUSB 2022 receipts/employment at 332439); class <5: 78 firms, avg revenue/firm $789,667 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $154,933 -> out of $1-10M band; class 5-9: 43 firms, avg revenue/firm $1,614,837 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $316,831 -> out of $1-10M band; class 10-19: 39 firms, avg revenue/firm $3,062,769 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $600,915 -> out of $1-10M band; class 20-99: 48 firms, avg revenue/firm $10,641,833 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $2,087,928 -> IN $1-10M band; class 100-499: 18 firms, avg revenue/firm $41,264,722 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $8,096,139 -> IN $1-10M band; class 500+: 17 firms, avg revenue/firm $182,334,588 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $35,774,046 -> out of $1-10M band; n_band = 66

caveats: The estimate includes elimination by alternative formats and reuse, not only software self-service.
A shift to another manufacturer does not reduce operator requirement even if it harms an incumbent.
o.change_evidence: Customer packaging-roadmap interviews and shipment data measuring conversion to plastic or fiber, IBC and bulk systems, closed-loop reuse, reconditioning rates, and insourced container production would materially change operator requirement.
o.rationale: Industrial customers still require an accountable manufacturer for physical, specification-compliant containers and hazardous-material performance. Some current quantity can be eliminated through reusable or reconditioned drums, bulk containers, plastics, fiber, or customer packaging redesign, but software itself cannot supply the physical basket.

caveats: Greif is a global diversified supplier rather than a target-scale firm.
Capture varies by standard drum, specialty lining or closure, short-run pail, and customer concentration.
q.change_evidence: A representative sample of LMM master supply agreements and purchase orders showing metal escalation, productivity clauses, rebates, rebid frequency, open-book rights, freight treatment, and realized post-improvement margins would materially change retention.
q.rationale: Recurring supply relationships, quality qualifications, freight, and local service let a manufacturer retain part of an operating improvement. Price-sensitive competition, steel-index effects, purchase-order contracting, volume rebates, customer procurement, and substitutable plastic or fiber packaging pass a meaningful share to buyers over five years.

caveats: General manufacturing surveys may not represent smaller drum and pail manufacturers.
Some operational improvements attributed to AI may actually be conventional automation or lean manufacturing.
rho.change_evidence: Verified deployments in independent industrial-container plants showing adoption, integration cost, false rejects, scrap, downtime, changeover time, labor hours, and customer acceptance would materially move this range.
rho.rationale: Repetitive lines and recurring specifications support five-year implementation in inspection, scheduling, predictive maintenance, inventory, order processing, and documentation. Legacy equipment, thin LMM data teams, integration cost, hazardous-goods quality requirements, customer approval, and limited downtime constrain scaling.

s5.bridge: Gallup measures stated five-year sale or transfer plans among US employer-business owners across industries, with 22% reporting such plans. I shade the base slightly downward for the gap between intention and a completed qualifying control transfer, while retaining an upper bound for aging owners and industry consolidation. The proxy may overstate completion or understate unsolicited strategic deals.
caveats: Gallup is cross-industry and measures owner plans rather than completed control transfers.
Some sales are individual plants or assets rather than transferable lens firms.
s5.change_evidence: Five years of completed private transactions in steel drums, barrels, and pails from PitchBook, GF Data, Axial, industrial-packaging advisers, and trade publications, divided by a verified eligible-firm denominator, would materially change this probability.
s5.rationale: National succession pressure and the presence of smaller competitors support recurring transfer opportunities. Customer concentration, environmental diligence, plant assets, commodity cycles, and strategic-buyer selectivity can prevent a planned exit from becoming a qualifying transaction.

principal_driver: Repeat consumption of specification-critical industrial packaging supports durable customer relationships and process data.
principal_weakness: The NAICS code is heterogeneous and the coherent industrial slice faces cyclical volume plus substitution by plastics, fiber, bulk formats, reuse, and reconditioning.
